marketState: neutral bearish — extreme heat priced in but storage surplus and weak 4-week seasonal odds cap upside
summary: Front price $3.279 is $0.32 above the seasonal fair value of $2.960 but remains within the fair-value band ($2.46–$3.46). Near-term weather demand is genuinely extraordinary — both the 1–7 day and 8–16 day outlooks are above the 5-year historical maximum, with current demand tracking +45% vs the 5-year normal, providing strong near-term price support. Against this, storage stands 151 Bcf above the 5-year average, a persistent bearish structural overhang reinforced by a 6 Bcf above-consensus injection this week. Seasonal odds are clearly bearish: only 19% of years see prices higher 4 weeks out with a typical 4-week move of –1.8%, and the near-curve structure (Aug > Sep) confirms the market views the current heat premium as temporary. COT positioning data was unavailable, limiting our ability to assess speculative crowding; this reduces conviction for both trade ideas.

longTerm.stop: $3.52 (above fair-value band upper of $3.46, clears near-term heat spike risk)
longTerm.tp2: $2.80 (below fair value; full heat normalization + storage overhang)
longTerm.rationale: Seasonal odds are stacked bearish: the 4-week probability of a higher price is only 19% with a typical –1.8% move, and the fair-value model shows the current $0.32 premium eroding as the fair-value midpoint itself drifts lower over the next 7–14 days before recovering only partially by week 3. Storage at 151 Bcf above the 5-year average — reinforced by a bearish +6 Bcf injection surprise — provides a structural ceiling on prices even if summer heat extends into July. The near-month curve (Aug slightly above Sep and Oct) confirms the market views current tightness as transient, while the steep winter strip (Jan 2027 $4.43 vs Aug $3.28) implies no structural demand pull in the near-term strip.
longTerm.invalidation: Storage builds come in significantly below 5-year averages for two or more consecutive weeks, demand remains above the 5-year historical maximum beyond mid-July, or price closes above $3.55 on a daily basis.

shortTerm.rationale: Both the 1–7 day and 8–16 day demand outlooks are above the 5-year historical maximum — sustained above-max heat could push price toward the $3.44–$3.46 fair-value band upper before seasonal gravity reasserts, making a fresh short unattractive at current levels. The 5m and 15m RSI has already fallen to oversold territory (34–34.5) while the 4h chart remains bullish (positive MACD, 50>200 EMA), signalling a likely near-term intraday bounce. Entering short into an oversold market with record demand and no clear weather inflection point is a poor-probability setup.
shortTerm.trigger: Enter short when EITHER (a) price rallies to $3.40–$3.46 (upper fair-value band) while 1–7 day demand visibly moderates below +30% vs 5-year average, OR (b) price breaks and closes below $3.18 on a daily basis, confirming seasonal gravity has taken hold ahead of the heat subsiding.

keyDrivers: Near-term weather demand above the 5-year historical maximum for BOTH the 1–7D and 8–16D horizons (+45% vs 5y normal) — dominant short-term price support
Storage 151 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +6 Bcf injection surprise this week — structural ceiling on any sustained rally
Seasonal 4-week probability only 19% for higher prices; typical 4-week move –1.8% in the cooling season
Price $0.32 above fair value ($2.96) but within band; fair value itself declines over the next 7–14 days before partially recovering, eroding the current premium
Near-month curve backwardation (Aug > Sep > Oct) confirms market treats current heat premium as transient; strong winter strip (Jan 2027 $4.43) reflects demand expectations beyond the near term
risks: Prolonged extreme heat beyond mid-July (8–16D demand already above 5y max) could delay the seasonal decline and push price to the $3.46 fair-value band upper or beyond
COT data unavailable — speculative short positioning unknown; a crowded short unwind could produce a sharp squeeze if weather repeatedly surprises to the upside
September is historically the strongest month in the cooling season (+3.1%); an early-September strength move could undermine short positions entered in late July or August
